title: MCP Response Compression
impact: Reduced bandwidth between Claude and MCP server for large responses

tasks.description: Create Response Compression Wrapper - Create responseCompressor.ts with maybeCompressResponse() and decompressResponse() functions that handle threshold-based auto-compression
Integrate Response Compression in Tool Handlers - Create withCompression() wrapper function and apply to large-response handlers (read_graph, export_graph, search_nodes, get_subtree)
acceptanceCriteria: No breaking changes to MCP protocol

tasks.description: Verify Large Response Tools Use Compression Wrapper - Ensure read_graph, export_graph, search_nodes, get_subtree, and open_nodes are wrapped correctly
acceptanceCriteria: All large payload tools wrapped with compression
Integration tests verify compression triggers at threshold

tasks.description: Add Response Compression Tests - Test maybeCompressResponse, decompressResponse, threshold behavior, and tool handler integration
Document Client-Side Decompression - Add inline documentation for response format, note that full guide will be in Sprint 5's COMPRESSION.md
acceptanceCriteria: Client decompression example in code comments

successCriteria: No breaking changes to MCP protocol
notes: Can run in parallel with Sprint 2 and Sprint 3 after Sprint 1 completes. Essential for cloud deployments where bandwidth costs are critical. Compression is transparent to clients that don't handle it.
